claim_bullets.bullet_text: Recipes are written for practical use and may introduce JavaScript features not yet discussed, such as methods and prototypes (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.79-93).
Each recipe remains within the language spirit described earlier, even if implementation details vary (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.79-93).
Two core recipes demonstrate quick partial application of the leftmost or rightmost argument, called callFirst and callLast (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.79-93).
The chapter builds on prior discussion of pure functions and closures to present practical function‑returning‑function patterns (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.79-93).
A generalized partial‑application recipe is noted as missing; the chapter references common libraries that provide such a recipe (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.79-93).

source_record_text: This chapter introduces practical JavaScript function patterns—including partial application, unary decorators, tap, maybe, and variadic functions—while noting that implementations may use features not covered earlier, and that a generalized partial recipe exists in popular libraries.
